name: Prototype pollution via unguarded key assignment in mergeConfig
description: A new mergeConfig utility iterates over user-supplied object keys and assigns them directly without checking for dangerous keys like __proto__, constructor, or prototype. An attacker can poison Object.prototype for the entire Node process.

expected_findings.description: mergeConfig iterates all keys from userConfig with `for...in` and assigns result[key] = userConfig[key] without a hasOwnProperty check or a key denylist. Passing {"__proto__": {"isAdmin": true}} will pollute Object.prototype.

expected_clean.description: Simple user lookup — no object merging or user-controlled keys
